mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5%, deteriorating labor income expectations drive forced de-risking and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and payroll employment indicate a synchronized supply-labor contraction that amplifies recession fears, driving negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in INDPRO and PAYEMS fail to predict negative 60-day returns with walk-forward hit rate below 55 percent or Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, a rebound in housing starts predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns as construction activity leads the cyclical trough.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST during post-shock recovery periods coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in industrial production reflects deteriorating corporate revenue visibility, mechanically lowering earnings expectations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO do not coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45 percent of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year CPI inflation below 1.5% signals demand deficiency and impending margin compression; once this threshold is breached, equity returns turn negative over the next 60 trading days as pricing power collapses.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL year-over-year below 1.5 percent coincides with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in non-farm payrolls signals direct income loss and consumption decline, leading to negative 5-day S&P 500 returns as labor market surprises are rapidly priced in.
failure_condition: Month-over-month contractions in PAYEMS fail to produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45 percent of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly contraction in real GDP reflects economy-wide demand destruction that equity investors continue to reprice lower, producing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns as the macro slowdown is confirmed.
failure_condition: Quarterly contractions in GDPC1 coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45 percent of walk-forward observations.
